findings.title: DIRECTIVE-20260526-002 executed — all 4 steps completed. PROP-062 authored.
findings.details: Per-entry diagnosis identified four distinct defect sub-classes (D-deletion-inverse, D-html-tag-split, O-non-standard-id+M-missing-pattern, D-id-collision+misrouted-closure). Of those, the first two are downstream of PROP-059's fingerprint guidance gaps; the third is pre-PROP-059 legacy; the fourth is an orthogonal ID-collision class out of scope for PROP-062 (flagged for possible PROP-063). Operator applies the per-entry recommendations individually — directive's non_goals forbid batch-execution.

findings.title: Verifier silent-skip population is large (1,589 entries) but mostly historical and not in the checked queue
findings.details: closed-issues.json has 2,112 total entries; 1,765 with status='fixed'; of those, 1,589 have no verification_pattern. The hourly verify-pending-state.js only checks entries with status matching /-pending-verification$/, so the 1,589 silent-skips don't generate noise. Only the 4 entries with status='fixed-pending-verification' AND defective patterns surface in the steady-state report. PROP-059 (2026-05-25) is working: only 1 of the 1,589 silent-skips was closed after PROP-059 deployment, and PROP-059's integrity-audit Step (change_2) would catch new silent-skips at the next integrity scan.
findings.recommendation: No structural action. The 4 directive entries are the actionable subset. The 1,589 historical silent-skips don't need a sweep — they don't generate hourly noise and going retroactively after them would multiply false-positive risk.

findings.title: 29 duplicate IDs in closed-issues.json — 4 IDs (ISS-2120/2121/2122/2123) have 3 entries each, 25 IDs have 2 entries each
findings.details: ISS-2122 specifically has 3 distinct bug-reports sharing the same `id` field, with closure_reason text on entry 1 that describes entry 2's bug (misrouted closure). Same shape likely affects ISS-2120/2121/2123. The duplicate-2 IDs (ISS-1423, ISS-1819, ISS-1834, ISS-1857..1868, ISS-1864..1868, ISS-1922 etc., 25 total) are mostly centered on the 2026-05-18 REPAIR cycle — workspace-sync deletion-disaster aftermath that may have re-inserted entries already present in git. Recommend operator file PROP-063 to investigate decider's next-ID generator collision and the 2026-05-18 REPAIR-cycle dedup gap.
findings.recommended_followup: PROP-063 (decider next-ID intake collision fix + retroactive dedup of the 29 duplicate-ID entries)
